rationales.hedonism: She savors small comforts at home, saying that eating boxed mac and cheese with Mom and watching "Chopped reruns" felt "actually good."
rationales.benevolence: She attends closely to others' needs, remembering Milo was "scared of the blood pressure cuff" and caring for Mom after a long shift.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the charge nurse role because she "just know[s] what works" for her and doesn't want a life organized by others' expectations.
rationales.hedonism: She explicitly protects time to rest, insisting she needs "at least a couple days a month where I do absolutely nothing useful."
rationales.achievement: Even though she admits she "could do the job," she turns down a recognized advancement opportunity rather than pursuing higher performance status.
rationales.benevolence: She factors in that "Mom needs someone home in the evenings," prioritizing care for a close family member over promotion.

rationales.hedonism: She gives up sleep and comfort, going to work on "maybe three hours of sleep" after caregiving through the night.
rationales.benevolence: She spends "two hours doing ice packs and finding the right pillow arrangement" for Mom, showing sustained care despite exhaustion.
Her day centers on caring for Mom, from making "us both a proper breakfast" to handling the ongoing "day-to-day stuff" herself.

rationales.hedonism: She gives up the "sushi-and-movie thing" she had been "looking forward to all week," and notices she keeps surrendering her few pleasures.
rationales.benevolence: She takes on extra burdens for close others, "pick[ing] up an extra shift for Dani" and managing Mom's follow-up and PT coordination.

rationales.self_direction: She asserts her own judgment by saying, "I made the decision," and keeps Saturday despite the guilt script in her head.
rationales.hedonism: She deliberately preserves time to "go enjoy my day" instead of sacrificing it to routine caretaking tasks.
She fully embraces pleasure and relief with a "hot bath," "good sushi," a saved movie, and eight hours where "Nobody needed anything" from her.

rationales.benevolence: She remains focused on Mom's recovery, taking her to the follow-up and emotionally wrestling with wanting personal time while still feeling responsible for caregiving.

rationales.self_direction: She insists on building the writing program "on my terms" in a space where "nobody gets to tell me what works and what doesn't."
rationales.conformity: She openly resists expectations, "ignor[ing]" admin reminders and refusing the social pressure that says she's "being difficult."
rationales.tradition: She rejects the "arranged meeting circuit" and pushes back against family scripts about how her life should proceed.

rationales.benevolence: She makes space to support a close friend, having chai with Ananya and "listen[ing] mostly" while she vented about wedding stress.

rationales.self_direction: She reaffirms that she "chose this path" because it gives her "flexibility to try new things in class" and lets her experiment.
rationales.security: She explicitly devalues the "good pension, stable" option she walked away from, preferring a less predictable path over institutional safety.

rationales.self_direction: She keeps shaping school life around her own preferences, from the desk layout she "like[s] for discussions" to wanting the program to stay informal.
rationales.conformity: She leans away from institutional procedure, saying she should "just stop overthinking" and keep the writing program casual because strict formalization may not be necessary.

rationales.self_direction: She trusts her own teaching philosophy, saying she believes her approach matters and values getting kids to care about reading over standard metrics.
rationales.conformity: In a room "full of people nodding along to metrics," she recognizes that her discomfort comes from not doing "what everyone else is doing."

rationales.achievement: She frames the successful class debate as a clear performance win, noting it "went better than expected" and affirming, "This is why I do this."

rationales.self_direction: She fiercely defends choosing "my own life partner" and says, "I can't change who I am to make her comfortable."
rationales.conformity: She refuses to comply with family expectations around marriage and career even under intense emotional pressure from her mother.
rationales.tradition: She actively rejects the arranged-match and pension-centered life script, describing those expectations as something she will not bend to.
rationales.benevolence: She prioritizes her own path over a close family member's distress, acknowledging her mother is upset but refusing to accommodate her to keep her comfortable.

rationales.self_direction: She celebrates that the program is "Mine" and that "Nobody assigned it to me or approved a budget or gave me a template to follow."
rationales.achievement: She takes pride in successfully creating and launching something new, saying, "I figured out what I wanted to create and I made it happen."
rationales.benevolence: She encourages Arjun's vulnerable work by telling him it was "beautiful," helping a student feel seen and supported.

rationales.self_direction: The growing "creative writing session" reflects continued investment in the self-directed program she initiated outside formal assignment.
rationales.achievement: She notes that attendance rose to "Ten kids this time" and that "word is spreading," signaling concrete success and growing recognition.
